English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell Rankhandi project records
Cornell University. Department of Anthropology
14-25-2787
52.7 cubic feet. 52.7 cubic feet.
1953-1984. Photographs, motion picture film, audiotapes, maps, subject files, index cards, field notes Field notes, subject files, cross index cards, surveys, photographs, movie films, audiotapes, maps, census data, and other documentary and research material of the Cornell Rankhandi Project. The project used the control Muslim village of Jhabiran in its study, and embraced the disciplines of anthropology, religion, sociology, political science, economics, linguistics, child psychology, history, and social psychology. Team members include Michael Mahar, Pauline Kolenda, Allan Atwell, John Gompers, John T. Hitchcock (Project Director), Robert T. Smith, Morris E. Opler, and Pat Hitchcock. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 56 57 58 59 61 (unprocessed) 62 (unprocessed) 63 (unprocessed) 64 (unprocessed) 65 (unprocessed) 66 (unprocessed) 60 (unprocessed) 67 (unprocessed) ======= 56 57 58 59 61 (unprocessed) 62 (unprocessed) 63 (unprocessed) 64 (unprocessed) 65 (unprocessed) 66 (unprocessed) 60 (unprocessed) 67 (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2627359
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Phil McCray, et al.
Date completed:
2004
EAD encoding:
Julia ParkerPeter Martinez, April 2004
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Field notes, subject files, cross index cards, surveys, photographs, movie films, audiotapes, maps, census data, and other documentary and research material of the Cornell Rankhandi Project. The project used the control Muslim village of Jhabiran in its study, and embraced the disciplines of anthropology, religion, sociology, political science, economics, linguistics, child psychology, history, and social psychology. Team members include Michael Mahar, Pauline Kolenda, Allan Atwell, John Gompers, John T. Hitchcock (Project Director), Robert T. Smith, Morris E. Opler, and Pat Hitchcock.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Movie film (16mm) by Pat Hitchcock. Includes outtakes. Village of Rankhandi (also known as Khaalaapur). Morris E. Opler, from the Ford Foundation. North Indian village prior to development. US/India Joint University Venture (Lucknow and Cornell)1953-56. Disciplines of study include anthropology, sociology, political science, economics, linguistics (John Gompers), child psychology, history, social psychology. John T. Hitchcock, Project Director. Robert J. Smith.
Series II. Photographic Includes colored slides, black and white negatives in isinglass slips, 3x5 drawers. Years of study: 1954, 1984 (1968, 1978, 1984 - Smithsonian)
Series III. Field notes HRAF format, typed cross index cards.
Series IV. Black and white prints HRAF code, indexed with key
Series V. Allan Atwell files Contemporary art of India.
Series VI. Maps Regulation government maps, specialized maps (eg. every house, tree, well in a village, population 5000)
Series VII. Phonodiscs Language records, 78 rpm, archaic vocabulary, Basis of Gompers' introductory Hindi
Series VIII. Surveys Crop production and other surveys
Series IX. Jhabiran files Control village (Muslim) most others are Hindi. HRAF coded, includes photographs.
Series X. Census material Most comprehensive village study ever. All documented, statistics of health.
Series XI.Audiotapes Village dialect, village music
Cite As:
Cornell Rankhandi project records, #14-25-2787. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
The Cornell Rankhandi Project was a sociological and anthropological study of the North India village of Rankhandi (Khalapur) undertaken in conjunction with Lucknow University of India, with the additional participation of the Ford Foundation. The project used the control Muslim village of Jhabiran in its study, and embraced the disciplines of anthropology, religion, sociology, political science, economics, linguistics, child psychology, history, and social psychology.
Motion pictures (visual works). Audio tapes. Photographs. India -- Economic conditions. India -- Religion. India -- Language. Jhabiran (India). Khalapur (India). Rankhandi (India). Economics -- India. Political science -- India. Sociology -- India. Anthropology -- India. Opler, Morris E., 1907- Mahar, J. Michael Kolenda, Pauline. Atwell, Allan. Gompers, John. Hitchcock, John T. Smith, Robert T. Hitchcock, Pat. Lucknow University Ford Foundation
